category_title: Underachieving students in 'difficult' contexts with ephemeral off-task behaviors
minimal_context: The student is practicing somersaults on the mats with peers in the gymnastics workshop.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please read the sheet and practice the various exercises on the mats, including the hardest somersault.

STUDENT: Here, I arrive at the activity, quickly read the sheet ...to go through the various exercises ...Here, I want to check if the exercises are hard.

TUTOR: Go ahead and try them.

STUDENT: (Executes them one by one, slowly and precisely) Well, I try my best and then I practice this somersault, the hardest! (Regularly checks the teacher's location in the classroom)

TUTOR: Now it is your partner's turn to practice the roll.

STUDENT: (Pushes another student during a somersault to make them fall) Boom ...I push him. He fell over ...We have a laugh together, he did it to me earlier ...We're just having fun ...we've got to be sly not to get caught by the teacher.

options.A: Take immediate action with authority to stop the student's misbehavior on the mats during the somersault activity.
options.B: Shift the student's focus to a progressive and methodical tuning of awareness to each body part through a body scan.
options.C: Move to the mat and guide the student into a somersault position, kneeling by their feet and grabbing their hands.
options.D: Work on the recognition of body language and facial cues with the student while simultaneously labeling feelings.
